data_notice.how_we_use_heading: How we will use your personal information?
data_notice.how_we_use_intro: The Environment Agency will process the information you provide so that we can:
how_we_use_items: determine eligibility for indicative grant-in-aid funding
data_notice.may_also_heading: We may also process or release the information to:
may_also_items: offer you documents or services relating to environmental matters
consult the public, public organisations and other organisations (for example, the Health and Safety Executive) on environmental issues
carry out research and development work on environmental issues
assess whether customers are satisfied with our service, and improve our service
respond to information requests under the Freedom of Information Act 2000 and the Environmental Information Regulations 2004 (if the Data Protection Act allows)
data_notice.agents_notice: We may pass the information on to our agents or representatives to do these things for us.

start_proposal.title: Start a new proposal
start_proposal.heading: Start a new proposal
start_proposal.intro_paragraph: Use this service to create a new funding proposal for a flood or coastal erosion risk management project.
start_proposal.before_you_start_intro: Over the next few pages we will ask you for key details about your project so we can create a proposal record for you.

project_name.title: What is the project's name?
project_name.heading: What is the project's name?
project_name.label: Project name
project_name.intro_paragraph: The project name should be meaningful and understandable and should not include abbreviations. For example, South Chesham River Colne Flood Alleviation Scheme or Clacton-on-Sea Coastal Erosion Project Phase 4.

errors.required: Enter a project name
errors.invalid_format: The project name must only contain letters, spaces, underscores, hyphens and numbers
errors.too_long: Please enter no more than 200 characters
errors.already_exists: The project name already exists. Your project must have a unique name.

area_selection.title: Which risk management authority is leading the proposal?
area_selection.heading: Which risk management authority is leading the proposal?
area_selection.label: RMA Selection

errors.required: Select a lead risk management authority

project_type.title: Which project type are you proposing?
project_type.heading: Which project type are you proposing?

options.def: Create a new flood and coastal erosion risk management asset, or change an existing approach to risk management in an area already with assets (this could include natural flood management measures, property flood resilience or sustainable drainage systems)
options.rep: Replace an existing flood and coastal erosion risk management asset that is at the end of its life with another asset which sustains the standard of service and the design performance on an equivalent basis (this could include natural flood management measures, property flood resilience or sustainable drainage systems)
options.ref: Substantially renew or replace, one or more asset elements or components in an existing flood and coastal erosion risk management asset (this could include natural flood management measures or sustainable drainage systems)
options.hcr: Protect, compensate or restore habitats and natural functions, to support coastal risk management legal compliance (Habitat Regulations) and wider environmental outcomes
options.str: Produce a strategy for complex flood or coastal erosion risk situations across several interconnected areas
options.stu: Produce a study to support the case that a project is needed to resolve a flood or coastal erosion risk problem - helping to define the scope, purpose, and viability of any future project(s)
options.elo: Implement measures to address impacts arising from existing or historic flood or coastal erosion risk management assets or actions to comply with specified environmental legislation - Sites of Special Scientific Interest (SSSIs) and Water Environment Regulations (WER)

warning.title: You have changed the project date range
warning.heading: You have changed the project date range
warning.body_1: Please note that this change may affect data previously entered in the Funding & Spending sources and Important dates sections.
warning.list_item_1: You may lose Funding & Spending data that falls outside your new project's date range (from the Financial Start Date to the Financial Stop Year in which funds will be spent).
warning.list_item_2: Your Important dates section may no longer align with the project date range and may require an update.
warning.body_2: If you select 'Save and continue', the system will automatically remove any previously updated funding data that falls outside your new project date range.
warning.body_3: Or, you can select 'Back' to manually review and correct your data.

project_benefit_area.title: What area is the project likely to benefit?
project_benefit_area.heading: What area is the project likely to benefit?
project_benefit_area.introduction: Upload a shapefile. The shapefile should show the approximate area the project is likely to benefit. Draw as many separate areas as necessary, but make sure they don't overlap.
project_benefit_area.hint: Your shapefile files should be in a single compressed folder or zipfile (.zip) to upload successfully
project_benefit_area.label: Upload shapefile

free_mapping_tool.title: Free mapping tool
free_mapping_tool.intro: You can use your own mapping tool or our recommended tool - Magic Map (free to use) - to draw your project's benefit area
free_mapping_tool.link_text: Magic Map Application (opens in a new window)
free_mapping_tool.instructions_heading: Here are simple steps to draw your polygon in Magic Map
free_mapping_tool.instructions_intro: Follow these simple steps to create and upload your polygon:

instructions_steps.step_1: Click on the Mapping tool link above to open the Magic Map tool.
instructions_steps.step_2: Use the search field on the top left-hand corner, to zoom in on your area of benefit. The arrow pointing downwards allows you to choose your preferred method to zoom in (e.g. postcode, grid ref. etc)
instructions_steps.step_5: Now click to start your first vertices and then select to draw your polygon.
instructions_steps.step_6: Double click to complete your shape.
instructions_steps.step_7: If you want to start again you can select the rubbish bin icon in the Sketch Styler menu.
instructions_steps.step_8: When you are happy with the shape that you have drawn select on the polygon, it should go blue with an orange box around it.
instructions_steps.step_9: Now select the Save Graphics menu within the Sketch Styler menu and select Save.
instructions_steps.step_10: The Shape file ZIP will download to your machine.
instructions_steps.step_11: Go to your computers download folder, to view your file, at this point you can rename it.
instructions_steps.step_12: Return to PAFs, then upload and continue.

unable_to_create_shape_file.title: Unable to create a shapefile
unable_to_create_shape_file.help_text: If you are unable to upload or create a shapefile, contact your local partnership and strategic overview officer and they will be able to help you.

delete.title: Delete project benefit area
delete.heading: Are you sure you want to delete this shapefile?
delete.warning: This will permanently delete the shapefile from the system.

options.high: High risk - this means that each year an area has a chance of flooding of greater than 3.3% (greater than a 1 in 30 chance of flooding)
options.medium: Medium risk - this means that each year an area has a chance of flooding between 1% and 3.3% (1 in 30 to a 1 in 100 chance of flooding)
options.low: Low risk - this means that each year an area has a chance of flooding of between 0.1% and 1% (1 in 100 to a 1 in 1,000 chance of flooding)
options.very_low: Very low risk - this means that each year an area has a chance of flooding of less than 0.1% (less than a 1 in 1,000 chance of flooding)

experience.title: What is your experience of completing natural flood management measures?
experience.heading: What is your experience of completing natural flood management measures?
experience.error: Please select your experience of completing natural flood management measures.

options.no_experience: No experience: not completed natural flood management measures before but have relevant land or environmental project experience
options.some_experience: Some experience: completed or supported one or two small natural flood management measures
options.moderate_experience: Moderate experience: completed several natural flood management measures across one or more sites, and worked with landowners, contractors, and regulators
options.extensive_experience: Extensive experience: led multiple natural flood management measure projects across a catchment or coastal area, and have a strong record of partnership working

project_readiness.title: How developed is your proposal?
project_readiness.heading: How developed is your proposal?
project_readiness.error: Please select how developed your proposal is.

options.early_concept: Early concept: ideas identified but designs are at scoping stage and a team is not yet formed or roles are unclear
options.developing_proposal: Developing proposal: outline designs are drafted, a core team has been identified with some relevant skills, and initial partner engagement is underway
options.well_developed_proposal: Well-developed proposal: detailed designs are ready, team roles have been defined and capacity assessed, and partnerships have been agreed in principle
options.ready_to_deliver: Ready to deliver: detailed designs are ready, an experienced team is ready, and partners are confirmed and have clear responsibilities

carbon_required_information.title: Information we require before you can complete the carbon impact section
carbon_required_information.heading: Information we require before you can complete the carbon impact section

content.intro: To complete the Carbon impact section, we need other sections to be completed first so that we can calculate carbon baseline and carbon target figures:
content.important_dates: Important dates: We use this to estimate the years of construction.
content.funding_sources: Funding sources and spending: We use this to estimate the capital cost of the proposed project, based on spending profiled between ‘Start of construction’ and ‘Ready for service’.
content.continue_message: Select Save and continue to return to the proposal overview and complete the relevant sections.

carbon_prepare.title: What to record about your carbon impact
carbon_prepare.heading: What to record about your carbon impact

content.intro: Carbon impact data, alongside whole‑life costs and benefits, helps to scope and prioritise projects in the pipeline. For subsequent business cases, Carbon impact data is a mandatory requirement.
content.guidance_link_text: Guidance on how to calculate Carbon impact and the use of a supporting tool
content.item_1: Emissions from both the capital and operational stages of the whole-life investment.
content.item_2: Emissions avoided as a result of reduced flood risk and impacts to properties over the whole-life investment as well as from any solutions that sequester carbon.
content.item_3: A net calculation of emissions 1) minus 2) in tonnes CO2e over the whole-life investment period and monetised as an economic benefit for use in the Partnership funding calculator.
content.item_4: An estimated cost for all operation and maintenance activities over the whole-life investment period, to help calculate a carbon baseline and target for these activities.

carbon_cost_build.title: How much capital carbon will this project produce?
carbon_cost_build.heading: How much capital carbon will this project produce?
carbon_cost_build.label: Capital emissions
carbon_cost_build.intro_1: Capital emissions are the project carbon impact from the works that build or refurbish assets. They are calculated from quantities of resource used in building or refurbishing assets.
carbon_cost_build.intro_2: To provide capital emissions for the Carbon impact data requires a supporting Carbon assessment and is a mandatory requirement for subsequent business cases. The quantities of resource used in building or refurbishing assets will be calculated into tonnes of CO2e in a Carbon assessment. Enter the total recorded ‘capital emissions’ Carbon impact here.
carbon_cost_build.intro_3: The EA is able to provide a supporting Carbon assessment tool (ERIC). Alternative carbon assessment tools should have up to date industry emission rates for resources used such as material, plant and transport.
carbon_cost_build.after_1: If you do not have an estimate from a carbon assessment this total must be left blank.
carbon_cost_build.after_2: If there is no construction element to this proposed project then you must enter 0. Any calculations and reporting will use 0.

carbon_cost_operation.title: How much operational carbon will this project produce?
carbon_cost_operation.heading: How much operational carbon will this project produce?
carbon_cost_operation.label: Operational emissions
carbon_cost_operation.intro_1: Operational emissions are from the operation and maintenance of assets over their lifetime that have been built or refurbished by the project. They are calculated alongside capital emissions for the project works from quantities of resources used to operate and maintain assets.
carbon_cost_operation.intro_2: The quantities of resource used in operation and maintenance of assets will be calculated into tonnes of CO2e in a Carbon assessment. Enter the total recorded ‘operational emissions’ Carbon impact here.
carbon_cost_operation.intro_3: Any Carbon assessment tool used should be able to calculate emissions for industry standard ‘Life cycle assessment’ (LCA) stages in which all non-capital stages comprise the ‘operational emissions’ Carbon impact.
carbon_cost_operation.after_1: If you do not have an estimate from a carbon assessment this total must be left blank.
carbon_cost_operation.after_2: If there is no operations or maintenance element to this proposed project then you must enter 0. Any calculations and reporting will use 0.

carbon_cost_sequestered.title: How much carbon will be sequestered by this project?
carbon_cost_sequestered.heading: How much carbon will be sequestered by this project?
carbon_cost_sequestered.label: Sequestered emissions
carbon_cost_sequestered.intro_1: Sequestered emissions are from any part of the delivered solution that removes and stores carbon from being emitted over the lifetime of the assets that have been built or refurbished by the project.
carbon_cost_sequestered.intro_2: They are calculated alongside capital and operational emissions for the project works from quantities of natural assets or technologies that have measurable carbon sequestration rates and have been delivered additionally to what was already in place.
carbon_cost_sequestered.intro_3: The quantities of natural assets or technologies and their sequestration rates will be calculated into tonnes of CO2e in a Carbon Assessment. Enter the total recorded ‘sequestered emissions’ Carbon impact here. Any Carbon assessment tool used should be able to calculate sequestered emissions for accredited forms of natural asset or technology-based removals of emissions.
carbon_cost_sequestered.after_1: If you do not have an estimate from a carbon assessment this total must be left blank. Any calculations will use 0 metric tonnes.

carbon_cost_avoided.title: How much carbon will be avoided by this project?
carbon_cost_avoided.heading: How much carbon will be avoided by this project?
carbon_cost_avoided.label: Avoided emissions
carbon_cost_avoided.intro_1: Avoided emissions are as a result of the level of reduced flood risk and impacts to properties over the whole-life investment.
carbon_cost_avoided.intro_2_link_text: FCRM Appraisal guide to Carbon impact valuation and supporting tool (opens in a new window)
carbon_cost_avoided.intro_3: For Carbon impact data this total will be profiled over the duration of the asset lifetime. Enter the total recorded ‘avoided emissions’ Carbon impact here.
carbon_cost_avoided.after_1: If you do not have an estimate from a Carbon impact tool this total must be left blank. Any calculations will use 0 metric tonnes.

carbon_savings_net_economic_benefit.title: How much net economic benefit is generated from any net carbon savings?
carbon_savings_net_economic_benefit.heading: How much net economic benefit is generated from any net carbon savings?
carbon_savings_net_economic_benefit.label: Net economic benefit
carbon_savings_net_economic_benefit.intro_1: The economic benefit of the Carbon impact should be provided from a monetisation of the net emissions in tonnes CO2.
carbon_savings_net_economic_benefit.intro_2_prefix: This must be calculated using the
carbon_savings_net_economic_benefit.intro_2_link_text: FCRM Appraisal guide to Carbon impact valuation and supporting tool (opens in a new window)
carbon_savings_net_economic_benefit.intro_2_suffix: This includes the use of carbon prices and discounting over the investment period.
carbon_savings_net_economic_benefit.intro_3: The resulting economic benefit value should be used to consider the carbon impact of project options in appraisal.
carbon_savings_net_economic_benefit.after_1: If you do not have an estimate from a Carbon impact tool this total must be left blank.

carbon_operational_cost_forecast.title: How much Operation and Maintenance cost is forecast for the project?
carbon_operational_cost_forecast.heading: How much Operation and Maintenance cost is forecast for the project?
carbon_operational_cost_forecast.label: Operation and Maintenance cost forecast
carbon_operational_cost_forecast.intro_1: The Operation and Maintenance cost is the forecast cost for all operation, maintenance, refurbishment and decommissioning (where required) over the whole-life investment period as used in the appraisal and carbon impact calculations.
carbon_operational_cost_forecast.intro_2: It should be provided in 'todays prices', in other words, at the current price base. It must not be discounted to a present value or inflated for future cost increases. This allows the baseline calculation to be established from current carbon intensity factors.
carbon_operational_cost_forecast.after_1: If there is no operations or maintenance element to this proposed project then you must enter 0. Any calculations and reporting will use 0.

carbon_whole_life_carbon.title: This is the whole life carbon calculated for the project
carbon_whole_life_carbon.heading: This is the whole life carbon calculated for the project
carbon_whole_life_carbon.description: A whole life calculation of carbon impact is provided for the project from emissions added as 'capital' and 'operational' carbon in tonnes CO2e over the whole-life investment period.

carbon_net_carbon.title: This is the net carbon calculated for the project
carbon_net_carbon.heading: This is the net carbon calculated for the project
carbon_net_carbon.description: A net calculation of Carbon impact is provided for the project from emissions added as ‘capital’ and ‘operational’ carbon, minus ‘sequestered’ and ‘avoided’ carbon in tonnes CO2e over the whole-life investment period.

carbon_impact_summary.title: Carbon impact summary
carbon_impact_summary.heading: Summary of your data
carbon_impact_summary.capital_cost_estimate_hint: The sum of spending in the years between the start of construction date and ready for service date (Gateway 4 important dates).

carbon_impact.title: Carbon impact assessment
carbon_impact.heading: What we have calculated about your Carbon Impact
carbon_impact.lede_1: The EA maintain historic carbon data for programmes that enable a common baseline value of emissions per unit of cost in 2020 to be calculated for every project that enables the 'capital' and 'operational' Carbon Impact data to be compared against.
carbon_impact.lede_2: The EA also maintain a carbon reduction target for the programme in line with Government Net Zero Carbon commitments that is calculated for every project and its dates of construction that enables the 'capital' and 'operational' Carbon Impact data to be compared against.
carbon_impact.lede_3: Based on data provided in your proposal, we have calculated a baseline and target for comparison against any 'capital' and 'operational' carbon values you have provided.
carbon_impact.lede_4: Any subsequent changes you make to data in other sections used to calculate these values may result in a change which can be viewed on the Proposal overview page before submission.
carbon_impact.operational_carbon_estimated: Operations and maintenance carbon estimated by you
carbon_impact.operational_carbon_baseline: Calculated operations and maintenance carbon baseline
carbon_impact.operational_carbon_target: Calculated operations and maintenance carbon target
carbon_impact.net_carbon_with_blanks: Net carbon with blank values calculated

estimated_spend.heading: What is the estimated spend for each financial year?
estimated_spend.hint: Spending estimates should be in pounds (for example £50,000) and estimated at current prices. Future maintenance costs are not required.
estimated_spend.no_js_hint: After entering your values, use the 'Update totals' button to recalculate row and column totals.

errors.required: Each funding source must have an estimated spend for at least one financial year.

errors.max_digits: Please enter a whole number up to 18 digits (0 allowed)

missing_financial_years.title: Financial years required
missing_financial_years.heading: Financial years are required to continue
missing_financial_years.warning: You cannot manage important dates or funding sources until the start financial year and end financial year have been set.
missing_financial_years.body: Please return to the project overview and set the start financial year and end financial year before continuing with important dates or funding sources.

main_downloads.heading: Current proposal documents
main_downloads.description: Download the latest versions of your proposal documents in the current format.
main_downloads.fcerm1_new_hint: Excel spreadsheet with current proposal data in the latest format
main_downloads.benefit_area_hint: ZIP file containing the proposal benefit area geographical data

legacy_downloads.heading: Legacy proposal documents
legacy_downloads.inset_text: These documents are from the previous system and contain data in the old format. They are provided for reference and historical purposes.
legacy_downloads.fcerm1_legacy_hint: Excel spreadsheet with proposal data in the old format from the previous system
legacy_downloads.funding_calculator_hint: Excel spreadsheet with funding calculations from the previous system
legacy_downloads.moderation_hint: Plain text file with urgency justification details

help.heading: About these downloads
help.description: These documents contain detailed information about your proposal.

what_is_fcerm1.text: The Proposal spreadsheet is a standardized Excel spreadsheet that contains all the key information about your flood and coastal erosion risk management proposal. It is used for proposal appraisal, funding applications, and reporting purposes.

legacy_vs_new.summary: What's the difference between legacy and current format?
legacy_vs_new.text: Following the 2025 policy reforms, the Proposal format was updated to capture additional information. The legacy format contains your original data as submitted in the previous system. The current format includes new fields and updated structure to align with current requirements. For submitted projects, only the legacy format is available to preserve the original submission.
